methodology: Cross-reference network (ISA.53.6→1PE.2.25=19v; ISA.53.4→1PE.2.24=14v; Lv16:21 50 total crossrefs) + patristic reading (Tertullian AD 220 'two goats=two natures'; Chrysostom 'bore to Father to FORGIVE'; Aquinas 'once for all'; 14 entries on Jn1:29) + CCEL (Edersheim 'sins confessed on LIVE goat'; Bushnell 'vicarious texture') + topic verification (SCAPEGOAT, ATONEMENT_DAY_OF) + ritual→prophecy→fulfillment chain mapping

evidence: commentaries (Haydock 'LXX: abandoned to our sins as to executioners')
commentaries (14 entries! Cyril 'Law typified in part; one Lamb died for ALL', Chrysostom 'shadow→substance')
commentaries (Chrysostom 'bore to Father to FORGIVE — not destroy but reconcile', Haydock 'EXHAUST the sins', Aquinas 'once for all perfected forever')

new_refs_added.justification: 14v de ISA.53.4. 15v→ACT.8.32. 11v→ISA.53.5-6. 14v→2CO.5.21. NT verse most directly echoing scapegoat language: 'He himself BORE our sins in his body on the TREE.' Combines carrying (scapegoat) + cross (sacrifice) in one verse.
3v de LEV.16.21. 43v→JHN.3.16(!). 14v de 1PE.2.24. Explicit forensic substitution: 'He who knew no sin was MADE SIN for us.' The 'laying on of hands' (Lv 16:21) in Pauline theology.

14v→1PE.2.24. 13v→MAT.8.17 (direct fulfillment citation). 69 total crossrefs — hub of Isaiah 53 network. Complements Is 53:6 with dimension of infirmity/sorrow: 'Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows.'

candidates_noted.justification: 9v de ISA.53.4. 14v→ROM.8.1. 11v→DEU.21.23. CURSE language echoes Tertullian's 'accursed' emissary goat.
3v de LEV.16.22. 'Bore the sin of many' = identical language to Heb 9:28.

two_goats_one_christ.source: Tertullian of Carthage (AD 220), Commentary on Leviticus 16:8
two_goats_one_christ.interpretation: Two goats represent two dimensions of Christ's work: (1) sacrificed goat = priestly sacrifice and death, (2) emissary goat = humiliation and passion. The emissary 'marked with all the signs of the Lord's passion: cursing, spitting, pulling, piercing.'
two_goats_one_christ.confirmed_by: Edersheim: 'sins confessed NOT on the dead goat but on the LIVE one (emissary).' Christ fulfills BOTH: dies (goat 1) AND bears sins away (goat 2) simultaneously.

chrysostom_inversion.description: Chrysostom inverts the destination: the OT goat carried sins to the wilderness (destruction/removal). Christ 'bore them TO THE FATHER — not to condemn but to FORGIVE.' The sins are not destroyed but reconciled.
chrysostom_inversion.significance: Transforms the scapegoat from a removal metaphor to a reconciliation metaphor — the most theologically sophisticated reading in this query.

chain.action: Laying on of hands → confession → transfer → goat bears sins to wilderness
'The LORD laid on him the iniquity of us ALL.' LXX: 'abandoned him to our sins as to executioners'
'Behold the Lamb of God who TAKES AWAY the sin of the world'
'Offered ONCE to bear the sins of many.' Chrysostom: 'bore to Father to forgive'

vocabulary_variation.significance: 6 different Portuguese constructions for the same concept of vicarious sin-bearing. Lexical search for 'bode' finds only Leviticus.
